rulings.Case Title: LYDIA BACA VS. MALINKA TACUMA MOYE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 30, 2023 line 1. DEFENDANT MALINKA MOYE'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND is OFF CALENDAR. No proof of service on file; motion filed 15 court days before the hearing date. Motion is not supported by a proper Notice of Motion or Points and Authorities setting forth the grounds and legal authority for relief requested. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: THE BANK OF NEW YORK MELLON, VS. THERESA LYNNE MURPHY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiff'S First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 30, 2023 line 4. DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA, N.A.'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT is DENIED. The paragraphs regarding the 2015 reconveyance and the request for attorney's fees apply to other Defendants. The paragraphs regarding punitive damages are supported by allegations of fraud. Plaintiff's request for judicial notice GRANTED. (Evid. Code, Section 452(d).) =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: THE BANK OF NEW YORK MELLON, VS. THERESA LYNNE MURPHY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 30, 2023 line 4. DEFENDANTS DANA MCGLOTHLIN, M.D. AND CHERILYN ELWELL, PHD.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is OVERRULED. The untimeliness of cause of action for judicial foreclosure does not clearly and affirmatively appear on the face of the FAC or the judicially noticed Notice of Default. (Austin v. Medicis (2018) 21 Cal.App.5th 577, 585.) Demurrer is not the appropriate procedure for determining the truth of disputed facts and holding an evidentiary hearing. (Joslin v. H.A.S. Ins. Brokerage (1986) 184 Cal.App.3d 369, 374.) 10 days to answer. Request for Judicial Notice of the Notice of Default is GRANTED. (Evid. Code, Section 452(h).) =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: THE BANK OF NEW YORK MELLON, VS. THERESA LYNNE MURPHY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 30, 2023 line 4. DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA, N.A.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part, and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer for the first cause of action for judicial foreclosure is overruled. It is not clear on the face of the complaint that the statute of limitations has run. Demurrer to the second cause of action, count one, for slander of title is overruled. Defendant Bank of America does not have standing. Demurrer to the second cause of action, count two, for slander of title, is overruled. The FAC alleges pecuniary loss. (FAC, 84, 86.) Demurrer to the third cause of action for cancellation of instruments is overruled. The FAC alleges the required elements. (U.S. Bank National Assn. v. Naifeh (2016) 1 Cal.App.5th 767, 778.) Demurrer to fourth cause of action for negligence is sustained, with leave to amend, for failure to plead the element of duty. There are no facts in the FAC supporting the claim that the transaction was intended to affect the plaintiff. (Seeley v. Seymour (1987) 190 Cal.App.3d 844, 861.) Plaintiff's request for judicial notice is GRANTED. (Evid. Code, Section 452(d).) =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: THE BANK OF NEW YORK MELLON, VS. THERESA LYNNE MURPHY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 30, 2023 line 4. DEFENDANT THERESA MURPHY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is OVERRULED. The untimeliness of cause of action for judicial foreclosure does not clearly and affirmatively appear on the face of the FAC. (Austin v. Medicis (2018) 21 Cal.App.5th 577, 585.) Demurrer is not the appropriate procedure for determining the truth of disputed facts and holding an evidentiary hearing. (Joslin v. H.A.S. Ins. Brokerage (1986) 184 Cal.App.3d 369, 374.) The cause of action for slander of title is not barred by the statute of limitations. (COVID-19 Emergency Rule, 9(a).) 10 days to answer. Defendant Murphy's request for judicial notice is GRANTED as to the complaint, answer, request to dismiss Defendant Murphy, first amended complaint, and amended motion for leave to file a first amended complaint. Request for judicial notice is denied as to the notice of default and notice of sale for lack of a declaration attesting to the veracity of the documents. Plaintiff's request for judicial notice GRANTED. (Evid. Code, Section 452(d).) =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: HERRICK CARVER VS. HVH HAYES STREET, LLC, ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 30, 2023 line 6. DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is DENIED. The claims, as alleged, arise out of the rent increase (not service of the notice of rent increase) and failure to investigate/confirm plaintiff's whereabouts, neither is a protected activity within the meaning of CCP 426.15. References to the service of the Notice of Rent Increase and the Rent Board proceeding do not form the basis of liability for any of the claims. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
